description_long: This is an elongated, wedge-shaped stone with a pointed tip and a broader, roughly rectangular base. The surface is irregular and textured, with visible natural undulations and a matte, dark greenish-brown coloration. One side of the stone features a small area with white painted or inscribed markings, possibly for cataloging or identification. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its edges are mostly rough and unpolished, suggesting minimal human intervention. The overall form is reminiscent of a primitive tool or a naturally fractured rock fragment.
description: A rough, wedge-shaped stone with a pointed tip, dark greenish-brown color, and visible surface texture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length and wedge shape, with the pointed tip and broad base clearly visible, as well as white markings on the base.
description_view_1: Edge-on view revealing the thinness of the stone and its irregular, uneven profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the stone's length and surface texture, with the pointed tip facing right and no visible markings.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again highlighting the stone's thin, tapered form and rough surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a small obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular natural mottling and surface undulations, with a small area of white painted or inscribed markings; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor surface pitting and natural texturing indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground cover or landscaping materials, but the white markings may create a slight visual contrast with its environment.
